records.ety: [OE. cler, cleer, OF. cler, F. clair, fr.L. clarus, clear, bright, loud, distinct, renowned; perh. akin to L. clamare to call, E. claim. Cf. Chanticleer, Clairvoyant, Claret, Clarify.]

defs: Free from opaqueness; transparent; bright; light; luminous; unclouded.
quotes: The stream is so transparent, pure, and clear.
Fair as the moon, clear as the sun.

defs: Free from ambiguity or indistinctness; lucid; perspicuous; plain; evident; manifest; indubitable.
quotes: One truth is clear; whatever is, is right.

defs: Able to perceive clearly; keen; acute; penetrating; discriminating; as, a clear intellect; a clear head.
quotes: Mother of science! now I feel thy power Within me clear, not only to discern Things in their causes, but to trace the ways Of highest agents.

defs: Not clouded with passion; serene; cheerful.
quotes: With a countenance as clear As friendship wears at feasts.

defs: Easily or distinctly heard; audible; canorous.
quotes: Hark! the numbers soft and clear Gently steal upon the ear.

defs: Without mixture; entirely pure; as, clear sand.
Without defect or blemish, such as freckles or knots; as, a clear complexion; clear lumber.
Free from guilt or stain; unblemished.
quotes: Statesman, yet friend to truth! in soul sincere, In action faithful, and in honor clear.

defs: Without diminution; in full; net; as, clear profit.
quotes: I often wished that I had clear, For life, six hundred pounds a-year.

defs: Free from impediment or obstruction; unobstructed; as, a clear view; to keep clear of debt.
quotes: My companion . . . left the way clear for him.

defs: Free from embarrassment; detention, etc.
quotes: The cruel corporal whispered in my ear, Five pounds, if rightly tipped, would set me clear.

defs: Full extent; distance between extreme limits; especially; the distance between the nearest surfaces of two bodies, or the space between walls; as, a room ten feet square in the clear.
In a clear manner; plainly.
quotes: Now clear I understand What oft . . . thoughts have searched in vain.

defs: Without limitation; wholly; quite; entirely; as, to cut a piece clear off.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cleared (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Clearing.]

defs: To render bright, transparent, or undimmed; to free from clouds.
quotes: He sweeps the skies and clears the cloudy north.

defs: To free from impurities; to clarify; to cleanse.
To free from obscurity or ambiguity; to relive of perplexity; to make perspicuous.
quotes: Many knotty points there are Which all discuss, but few can clear.

defs: To render more quick or acute, as the understanding; to make perspicacious.
quotes: Our common prints would clear up their understandings.

defs: To free from impediment or incumbrance, from defilement, or from anything injurious, useless, or offensive; as, to clear land of trees or brushwood, or from stones; to clear the sight or the voice; to clear one's self from debt; -- often used with of, off, away, or out.
quotes: Clear your mind of cant.
A statue lies hid in a block of marble; and the art of the statuary only clears away the superfluous matter.

defs: To free from the imputation of guilt; to justify, vindicate, or acquit; -- often used with from before the thing imputed.
quotes: I . . . am sure he will clear me from partiality.
How! wouldst thou clear rebellion?

defs: To leap or pass by, or over, without touching or failure; as, to clear a hedge; to clear a reef.
To gain without deduction; to net.
quotes: The profit which she cleared on the cargo.

defs: To become free from clouds or fog; to become fair; -- of the weather; -- often followed by up, off, or away.
quotes: So foul a sky clears not without a storm.
Advise him to stay till the weather clears up.

defs: To become free from turbidity; -- of solutions or suspensions of liquids; as, the salt has not completely dissolved until the suspension clears up; when refrigerated, the juice may become cloudy, but when warmed to room temperature, it clears up again.
To disengage one's self from incumbrances, distress, or entanglements; to become free.
quotes: He that clears at once will relapse; for finding himself out of straits, he will revert to his customs; but he that cleareth by degrees induceth a habit of frugality.

defs: To make exchanges of checks and bills, and settle balances, as is done in a clearing house.
To obtain a clearance; as, the steamer cleared for Liverpool to-day.
